title: Backend.AI Service Definition
description: Defines a service that can be launched inside a Backend.AI container. Stored as JSON files in /etc/backend.ai/service-defs/.

command.description: Command to start the service. Can be a string (executed via shell) or an array of arguments.

shell.description: Shell to use when command is a string.

noop.description: If true, the service is a no-op and will not actually start any process.

url_template.description: URL template for accessing the service. Supports variable interpolation with {host}, {port}, etc.

prestart.description: List of actions to execute before starting the service.

env.description: Environment variables to set for the service.

allowed_envs.description: List of environment variable names that users are allowed to override.

allowed_arguments.description: List of argument names that users are allowed to pass.

default_arguments.description: Default argument values. Each value can be null, a string, or a list of strings.

Action.description: A pre-start action to execute before the service starts.

action.description: The name of the action to execute (must be a function in service_actions module).

args.description: Keyword arguments passed to the action function.

ref.description: Variable name to store the action's return value for later interpolation.
